title: M/S. The Rajaji Nagar Housing ... vs Income Tax Officer, Ward-5(2)(3), ... on 30 April, 2024
body: M/S. The Rajaji Nagar Housing ... vs Income Tax Officer, Ward-5(2)(3), ... on 30 April, 2024 IN THE INCOME TAX APPELLATE TRIBUNAL "B" BENCH : BANGALORE BEFORE SHRI NARENDER KUMAR CHOUDHRY, JUDICIAL MEMBER AND SHRI LAXMI PRASAD SAHU, ACCOUNTANT MEMBER ITA No.89/Bang/2024 Assessment year : 2018-19 The Rajajinagar Housing Co-operative Vs. The Income Tax Officer, Society Ltd., Ward 5(2)(3), No.136/1, 17th Main Road, Bangalore. 47th Cross, 3rd Block, Rajajinagar, Bengaluru - 560 010. PAN : AAEAT 5387Q APPELLANT RESPONDENT Appellant by : None Respondent by : Shri Ganesh R. Ghale, Standing Counsel Date of hearing : 14.03.2024 Date of Pronouncement : 14.03.2024 ORDER Per Laxmi Prasad Sahu, Accountant Member This appeal is filed by the assessee against the order dated 23.11.2023 of the CIT(Appeals), National Faceless Appeal Centre, Delhi [NFAC], for the AY 2018-19. 2. The assessee filed return of income on 31.12.2018 declaring total income at Rs.27,910. The case was selected for scrutiny and statutory notices were issued and the assessee furnished details. The ITA No.89/Bang/2024 Page 2 of 5 AO accepted the returned income and completed assessment u/s. 143(3) on 2.3.2021. In the computation sheet, the AO computed total income of Rs.7,42,161 and raised tax demand of Rs.3,26,441. Aggrieved, the assessee filed appeal before the First Appellate Authority (FAA). 3. The assessee submitted in the Statement of Facts as per Form 35 that the return was processed u/s. 143(1) of the Act and disallowance u/s. 80P(2)(d) was made of Rs.7,14,251 and demand of Rs.3,26,441 was raised. Subsequently notice u/s. 143(2) was issued and assessment completed u/s. 143(3) accepting the returned income of Rs.27,910 but in the computation sheet demand of Rs.3,26,441 was raised as per the 143(1) order. The assessee raised a ground before the CIT(Appeals) that when a case is selected for scrutiny and notice u/s. 143(2) is issued, the intimation u/s. 143(1) is not sustainable. During the course of assessment u/s. 143(3), at no point of time the AO raised the question of delay in filing the return and about disallowance of income from other sources u/s. 80P(2)(d) of the Act. Hence the demand raised as per intimation u/s. 143(1) is not correct. 4. The CIT(Appeals) observed that intimation u/s. 143(1) was passed on 12.02.2019 whereas notice u/s. 143(2) of the Act was issued on 22.09.2019. If the assessee is aggrieved by the disallowance by the CPC vide intimation u/s. 143(1) , the assessee can file an appeal against the 143(1) intimation. The demand raised on completion of assessment u/s. 143(3) is on account of disallowance u/s. 80P(2)(d) of the Act in ITA No.89/Bang/2024 Page 3 of 5 the intimation but not from the assessment order. Thus assessee ought to have filed appeal against the intimation u/s. 143(1) dated 12.02.2019 from which the demand emanated and not against the 143(3) order. 5. On merits of the case, the CIT(Appeals) observed that the assessee is not eligible for deduction u/s. 80P(2)(d) of the Act of Rs.7,14,251 when the return of income is not filed within the due date as per section 80AC of the Act. The assessee filed return of income on 31.12.2018 after the due date specified u/s. 139(1) on 31.08.2018. Therefore the disallowance of claim of deduction u/s. 80P(2)(d) in the intimation u/s. 143(1)(a)(ii) is justified. Hence the CIT(Appeals) dismissed the appeal of the assessee. 6. The case was heard qua the assessee. The ld. DR submitted that the assessee has taken a ground challenging the addition made u/s. 143(1) in the appeal filed against the assessment order u/s. 143(3) r.w.s. 143(3A) & 143(3B) of the Act which is not sustainable. The proceedings u/s. 143(3) and 143(1) are quite different and addition made u/s. 143(1) can be challenged by way of separate appeal against that order before the FAA. 7. After considering the submissions of the ld. DR, we concur with the ld. DR. Once returned income filed by the assessee has been accepted by the AO in the assessment u/s. 143(3) , there should not be any grievance to the assessee against that order. The appeal filed by assessee before the CIT(Appeals) against the addition in the intimation u/s. 143(1) is not maintainable. The return was processed by the CPC ITA No.89/Bang/2024 Page 4 of 5 u/s. 143(1) and disallowance under Chapter VIA was made, against which the assessee should have filed appeal separately before the CIT(Appeals). The assessee is at liberty to file separate appeal against the intimation u/s. 143(1) before the CIT(Appeals). Accordingly we dismiss this ground of the assessee. 8. We further note that while computing the tax demand of Rs.3,76,441 by the AO, the total income has been considered at Rs.7,42,160. The AO has wrongly considered the tax demand u/s. 143(3) . He has clubbed the disallowance made u/s. 143(1) while computing tax liability u/s. 143(3) . We note that even there is no reference to disallowance made u/s. 143(1)(a) in the income and tax computation sheet. Accordingly, the demand raised by the AO u/s. 143(3) is wrong. 9. The assessee has submitted in the ground of appeal that return of income was filed beyond due date due to the negligence of the authorised person and it was beyond the control of the assessee society. It referred to the CBDT Circular No.13 of 2023 dated 26.07.2023 wherein in order to mitigate the genuine hardship in cases, the Chief Commissioners of Income Tax (CCsIT) / Directors General of Income Tax (DGsIT) are authorised to deal with such application of condonation of delay pending before the Board, upon transfer of such application by the Board, on merits. Further the assessee has submitted in the grounds of appeal that the CIT(A), NFAC has without giving any due consideration to the above Circular has passed the order, ITA No.89/Bang/2024 Page 5 of 5 which is misplaced. The Board has authorised CCIT/DGIT for consideration of application of condonation of delay on merits as provided in the above Circular. Therefore, in this regard, the assessee may move appropriate application for condonation of delay for belated filing of the return before the concerned authority if it has not filed. 10. In the result, the appeal by the assessee is partly allowed for statistical purposes. Pronounced in the open court on this 14th day of March, 2024, through virtual hearing. Sd/- Sd/- (NARENDER KUMAR CHOUDHRY) (LAXMI PRASAD SAHU ) JUDICIAL MEMBER ACCOUNTANT MEMBER Bangalore, Dated, the 14th March, 2024. /Desai S Murthy / Copy to: 1. Appellant 2. Respondent 3. Pr. CIT 4. CIT(A) 5. DR, ITAT, Bangalore. By order Assistant Registrar ITAT, Bangalore.
